meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve Can't Debug Code
content.seo_description: Learn how to master a professional client workflow to overcome debugging hurdles. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at red error logs in isolation. By adopting a structured client-side debugging workflow, you isolate logic failures from UI state. Use Antigravity to map your component dependencies and leverage Claude Code to perform automated root-cause analysis, turning frustrating syntax errors into clear, actionable refactoring paths for your project.
content.intro:
The screen is a blur of crimson text. You have spent hours chasing a bug that refuses to die, and the weight of 'I can't debug this' is starting to feel like a career wall. Every developer remembers this specific crucible—the moment where the code feels like an impenetrable fortress.

But here is the industry secret: debugging isn't about being a genius; it is about having a repeatable, systematic workflow. By offloading the mental load to AI-powered agents, you transition from 'guessing' to 'engineering.' It is time to stop fighting the machine and start orchestrating it.

steps.title: Define the Failure State
steps.description: Before fixing, you must articulate the divergence between expected output and actual behavior to ground the AI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current component state and compare it against the expected data schema. Identify the exact line where the state mutation deviates from the design intent.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Explain why the state in this component is failing to update correctly based on the provided logs.'

steps.title: Isolate the Logic Branch
steps.description: Use tooling to strip away irrelevant UI code and focus exclusively on the business logic layer causing the crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Extract the core logic block from this component and refactor it into a pure function for isolated testing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Extract the logic from src/components/DataHandler.tsx into a testable unit file.'

steps.title: Automated Traceback Analysis
steps.description: Pipe your error logs directly into your AI agent to correlate stack traces with your specific code implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the stack trace provided below and map it to the specific function calls in my codebase that triggered the exception.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Trace this error log back to the source code and suggest a fix for the null pointer exception.'

steps.title: Constraint-Based Refactoring
steps.description: Apply strict constraints to your code to prevent the error from resurfacing during future feature iterations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this module to include defensive programming patterns that validate inputs before execution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add comprehensive error handling and input validation to the current function.'

steps.title: Verification and Regression Testing
steps.description: Ensure the fix works and doesn't break existing features by running a targeted test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a unit test for this function that covers the edge case previously causing the crash.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Create a test file for the recently refactored module and verify it passes.'

pro_tips: Always comment your 'why' before the 'how' so the AI understands your intent, not just your syntax.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation for your fixes; it forces you to clarify your own understanding of the solution.
Treat the AI as a junior pair programmer: ask it to explain its reasoning, not just provide the final code snippet.
When a bug seems unsolvable, ask the AI to suggest three different architectural approaches to the problem to break your tunnel vision.
Keep your context window clean by deleting old, irrelevant log files before starting a new debugging session with Claude Code.
content.next_steps: After mastering this workflow, integrate a linting tool into your CI/CD pipeline to catch these errors before they even reach your console.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are an engineer with a system. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
